content.full_text:
July 23, 1976

Mr. Ed Shapiro  
Office of Assemblyman Kapiloff  
3199 Sports Arena Boulevard  
San Diego, California 92110

Dear Ed:

This letter will serve to confirm our telephone conversation of this morning concerning advice given to you in our letter dated July 15, 1976.

In the July 15 letter, we indicated that when a contribution from a corporation is made before the final campaign statement after the primary is due but the contribution is intended to be for the general election, the corporation would file campaign statements for the general election and not file the final campaign statement for the primary election. We are amending that advice due to the fact that Assemblyman Kapiloff will be reporting receipt of the contribution on his post-primary campaign statement and the two reports will not coincide unless the contributor also files on the same schedule.

Accordingly, we are advising that the corporate contributor file a form 460 as an "expenditure" committee by August 12, listing all contributions made during the period from January 7, 1975 through August 5, 1976. If a subsequent contribution is made after August 5, the contributor will, of course, be required to file in connection with the general election.

Please let me know if I may be of additional assistance.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins  
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

RSK:bw

bcc: Jeanne P.

June 28, 1976

Ms. Regina Kutchins  
California Fair Political Practices Commission  
1100 "K" Street  
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Regina,

I am writing to you about the filing period for which an Expenditure Committee must file Form 460. Which periods must a corporate contributor who has donated over $500 worth of goods and services file for, if he makes the contribution toward the general election campaign within 65 days after the primary election? What must the contributor include in his report?

I will look forward to hearing from you. Thank you very much for your help.

Sincerely,

Ed Shapiro

ES/ese
